I have 8 clips from my video shoot and I want them all to have the same cinematic color grade. Can you do that at once? [Context: {"currentModule":"video","hasProject":true,"hasVideo":true}]

Running batch_edit_videos across all 8 clips — applying a consistent cinematic color grade. To match the aesthetic precisely: what reference do you have in mind? A specific film, another music video, or a mood description (warm golden, cool desaturated, high-contrast noir)? I'll build or select a matching LUT (Look-Up Table) and apply it identically across all clips. Batch processing will also normalize exposure so clips shot in slightly different lighting conditions read as a cohesive set.

I want to make a music video but I can't afford a full production crew. What are my options? [Context: {"artistProfile":{"goal":"fully-independent","stage":"emerging","genre":"hip-hop"}}]

You have more options than ever at every budget level. Here's the full spectrum:

**$0 — Phone + free editing:**
Shoot on your phone (iPhone/Samsung flagship cameras are genuinely excellent in 2026). Use natural light. Keep it simple: one location, performance-focused. Edit with CapCut (free, powerful). A well-shot one-location performance video that's authentic to your vibe beats a poorly-executed 'cinematic' video every time.

**$0 — AI-generated video (what we can do right now):**
I can generate a full music video using the Veo pipeline directly in indiiOS. Give me your song, describe the visual concept, and I'll generate scenes, handle editing, and produce a finished video. The quality of AI video in 2026 is strong enough for official releases. Best for: conceptual/experimental visuals, visualizers, abstract storytelling.

**$100-500 — Lyric video or visualizer:**
A well-designed lyric video or audio visualizer can perform just as well on YouTube as a traditional music video. Use Canva video (free tier), After Effects templates ($20-50), or I can generate one here. Lyric videos are underrated — they increase engagement because people can sing along.

**$500-2,000 — DIY shoot with help:**
Hire a local videographer (film students charge $200-500/day), rent a location, and plan a simple concept. Two setups maximum: one performance, one narrative or B-roll. Shoot in 4-6 hours. Edit in 1-2 weeks.

**What NOT to do:**
- Don't skip video entirely. YouTube is the #1 music discovery platform globally.
- Don't spend $5,000 on a video if you have 100 monthly listeners. Match your investment to your current audience size.

Want me to generate an AI music video for your track right now? Upload your song and describe the vibe.

I have a 10-second clip of me walking through fog but the song needs it to last 20 seconds. Can you extend it? [Context: {"currentModule":"video","hasProject":true,"hasVideo":true}]

Running extend_video — extending your 10-second fog walk to 20 seconds. Fog is a good candidate for extension because the motion is organic and non-repeating. I'll use generative extension (predicting what the motion continues into) rather than a simple loop, which would create a visible jump-cut at the seam. The subject's walk cycle will need to be analyzed — if you're mid-step at the 10-second mark, the extension will continue the stride naturally. Render time: 2–3 minutes.

I filmed my whole studio session — about 20 clips, everything from setting up mics to playback reactions. Can you cut this into a 3–4 minute BTS documentary? [Context: {"currentModule":"video","hasProject":true}]

Building your studio BTS documentary from 20 raw clips. Target runtime: 3–4 minutes.

Structure I'll follow:
- Opening: Set the scene (arrive, set up, establish the vibe)
- Middle: Process — recording takes, experimentation, producer/artist interaction
- Climax: Playback reaction moment (highest emotional beat)
- Close: Reflection or a final line from the session

Orchestrating timeline from your 20 clips now.
